riddle:
1. Seek the page of the international organization that serves as the umbrella group for national hemophilia societies worldwide, supporting people with haemophilia and other inherited bleeding disorders, and record the year shown for when that organization was established.

2. There exists a small numerical twist: take the year you just noted and advance to the next prime number after it — that prime is your new value.

3. How many language links appear near the top of the general Wikipedia article that explains days set aside to raise public awareness (the page that defines such observances)? After you note that count, hunt for the New York City monument honoring early women’s-rights pioneers and open its page.

4. If you view the city entry for New York on a popular online map service, what star rating do travellers give that metropolis?

5. The article whose opening paragraph tells the story of a grassroots Manhattan health group formed in the early years of the AIDS crisis contains its founding year; locate that page and write down the year it was founded.

6. In the end, compute the remainder when (clue 1 + clue 2) is divided by 10; perform that arithmetic using the numeric values you obtained for clue 1 and clue 2, and give the single-digit result (0–9).

metadata.theme: From rare-disease advocacy to public recognition: how health organizations spark awareness days and civic memory
